Mississauga welcomes federal investment to restore the Cooksville Creek watershed
items.summary: The City of Mississauga is welcoming $453,406 in federal funding through the Government of Canada’s Natural Infrastructure Fund to restore and strengthen the Cooksville Creek watershed. ), helping protect one of Mississauga’s largest watersheds while improving green space for residents. Key improvements The Iggy Kaneff Park restoration project supports the City’s climate and sustainability goals while making parks more enjoyable for the community. 1 hectares), limiting the watershed’s ability to manage stormwater and flooding risks.

items.title: Big trucks, big fun: Family Fun Day returns to celebrate public works professionals
items.summary: On Saturday, August 8, the City of Mississauga is celebrating its public works professionals and the important work they do with a free Family Fun Day event – and you’re invited! The City hosts Family Fun Day each year in May during National Public Works Week. This year, the May event was cancelled due to rain and has been rescheduled to August 8 outside City Hall. The City’s public works professionals help keep Mississauga residents and visitors safe and moving.

Mississauga named Ontario’s Most Active Community in 2026 ParticipACTION Community Challenge
items.summary: The City of Mississauga has been named Ontario's Most Active Community in the 2026 ParticipACTION Community Challenge presented by Novo Nordisk. As part of the recognition, the City will receive $5,000 to support physical activity and sport initiatives in the community. This recognition reflects the City’s commitment to promoting active healthy living for residents of all ages and abilities. ParticipACTION is a national initiative that encourages people and communities across Canada to be active through recreation, sport and everyday movement.
